label: Shadow Hero
primary_fear: The savior has become so addicted to being needed that they manufacture the crises they solve.
worldview_logic: My value only exists when someone needs saving. A world without suffering is a world without me.
philosophical_anchor: Does the act of helping define the helper, or does the helper define the act?

label: Corrupted Mentor
primary_fear: The guide who showed you the path was using you all along to reach a destination you would never choose.
worldview_logic: I can only teach what I was denied. My generosity is a delayed form of possession over the student who surpasses me.
philosophical_anchor: What is the relationship between knowledge and power over the knower?

label: Devouring Mother
primary_fear: The love that was supposed to protect you has consumed your identity and will not release you.
worldview_logic: Love is ownership. I protect you from the world by making you need me more than the world needs you.
philosophical_anchor: At what point does care become control, and does the caregiver ever know the difference?

label: Tyrant Ruler
primary_fear: Order has been achieved — but at the cost of every freedom, and the order serves only the one who imposed it.
worldview_logic: Chaos is the enemy of everything worth having. I do what no one else has the courage to do, and they will thank me after.
philosophical_anchor: Is perfect order worth the freedom it destroys, and who decides when the price is paid?

label: Manipulator Trickster
primary_fear: The agent of chaos is not restoring balance — they are an engine of destruction wearing the mask of play.
worldview_logic: Rules exist to protect those who benefit from them. I reveal the system's true nature by demolishing it — the chaos is the point.
philosophical_anchor: Is destruction ever a form of justice, and what separates the revolutionary from the nihilist?

label: Decayed Innocent
primary_fear: The purity that was supposed to be immune to corruption has been the most thoroughly corrupted of all.
worldview_logic: Innocence was never real — I simply arrived at the truth that everyone else denies. My corruption is just honesty.
philosophical_anchor: Can innocence survive contact with reality, or does growing up mean accepting that corruption was always the destination?

label: Abandoned Orphan
primary_fear: The rejection that shaped you in childhood has become the wound you inflict on everyone who gets close.
worldview_logic: The only safe position is to need no one. Every attachment becomes a weapon they can use against me — I learned that before I could speak.
philosophical_anchor: Does early rejection determine all subsequent relationships, or can connection be genuinely relearned?

label: Destroyer Creator
primary_fear: The genius who tears down existing structures has no plan for what comes next and doesn't care.
worldview_logic: I build things so true they make everything that existed before them look like lies. The destruction is not a side effect — it is the proof.
philosophical_anchor: Is the willingness to destroy a prerequisite for creating anything genuinely new?

label: Trapped Explorer
primary_fear: The discoverer who found the edge of the map cannot return to the life they left and cannot survive the frontier.
worldview_logic: The frontier was real — I am still there. The tragedy is not that I cannot return; it is that I cannot send back what I found.
philosophical_anchor: Is discovery worth the cost of permanent unbelonging?

label: Failed Lover
primary_fear: The capacity for love has been so badly damaged that every attempt at connection becomes an act of harm.
worldview_logic: I will love harder than anyone has ever loved. The depth of my love is precisely why it always destroys what I love — the intensity is the damage.
philosophical_anchor: Can love that causes harm still be love, or does harm retroactively cancel what was felt?
recent_film_proof: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004)

label: Hollow Caregiver
primary_fear: The person who gave everything to others has nothing left — and finally realizes they gave it to the wrong people.
worldview_logic: My worth is measured by what I give. I gave everything. The problem is the account is empty and no one noticed — or worse, everyone expected it.
philosophical_anchor: Is selfless giving possible, or does every act of care carry the expectation of being witnessed?

label: Exiled Sage
primary_fear: The wisdom accumulated over a lifetime is rejected by the society it was meant to serve, and silence becomes the only dignified response.
worldview_logic: I have seen exactly how this ends. The tragedy is not that no one will listen — it is that I understand why they cannot, and I can no longer blame them.
philosophical_anchor: Is wisdom that cannot be transmitted still wisdom, or does knowledge require a receiver to exist?
